Name: Trade waste management and disposal for construction, alterations, and demolition.
Title: A Local Law to amend the administrative code of the city of New York, in relation to trade waste management and disposal for construction, alterations, and demolition
BodyName: Committee on Sanitation and Solid Waste Management

History.Description: This Introduction was Introduced by Council
This Introduction was Referred to Comm by Council to the Committee on Sanitation and Solid Waste Management

Summary: This bill would require the applicants for permits related to the construction of new buildings and the alteration or demolition of existing buildings to submit to the Department of Buildings (“DOB”) a construction waste management and disposal plan, outlining measures to ensure trade waste generated by such work is properly disposed. Prior to the sign-off of a permit, this bill would require DOB, in conjunction with the Department of Sanitation and Department of Environmental Protection, to verify the permit holder’s compliance with such plan. Finally, this bill would allow DOB to issue a minimum civil penalty of $1,000 for failure to comply with the plan, and allow DOB and the Department of Consumer and Worker Protection to suspend or revoke contractor licenses for repeated failure to adhere to such plans.

\ul\v0\par
Be it enacted by the Council as follows:\ulnone\par
\pard\ltrpar\fi720\qj\par
\pard\ltrpar\fi720\sl480\slmult1\qj Section 1. Section 20-392 of the administrative code of the city of New York, as amended by local law number 65 for the year 1997, is amended to read as follows:\par
\'a7 20-392 Fines; issuance, renewal, suspension\ul ,\ulnone  and revocation of license. a. The commissioner shall have the power to impose a fine not to exceed [one thousand dollars] \ul $1000\ulnone  upon a licensee [and/or] \ul or\ulnone  suspend or revoke a license\ul , or both,\ulnone  or deny an application for the issuance or renewal of a license for any [one] \ul 1\ulnone  or more of the following causes:\par
1. Fraud, misrepresentation, or bribery in securing a license[.]\ul ;\par
\ulnone 2. The making of any false statement as to a material matter in any application for a license[.]\ul ;\par
\ulnone 3. The person or the management personnel of the contractor are untrustworthy or not of good character[.]\ul ;\par
\ulnone 4. The business transactions of the contractor have been or are marked by a practice of failure to timely perform or complete its contracts, or the manipulation of assets or accounts, or by fraud or bad faith, or is marked by an unwholesome method or practice of solicitation of business from owners[.]\ul ;\par
\ulnone 5. Failure to display the license as provided in this subchapter[.]\ul ;\par
\ulnone 6. Failure to comply with any demand or requirement lawfully made by the commissioner[.]\ul ;\par
\ulnone 7. When an agent or employee of a licensee has been guilty of an act of omission, fraud, or misrepresentation and the licensee has approved or had knowledge thereof[.]\ul ;\par
\ulnone 8.  Violation of any provision of this subchapter or any rule or regulation adopted hereunder or for performing or attempting to perform any act prohibited by this subchapter[.]\ul ;\par
\ulnone 9. Violation of any provision of subchapters [one] \ul 1\ulnone , [two] \ul 2,\ulnone  or [three] \ul 3\ulnone  of title [twenty-six] \ul 26\ulnone  or subchapter [one] \ul 1\ulnone  of title [twenty-seven] \ul 27\ulnone  of this code, or any rule adopted thereunder or for performing or attempting to perform any act prohibited by such subchapters, provided that the commissioner shall suspend or revoke the license of any licensee who continues to work in violation of a [stop-work notice or] \ul stop work\ulnone  order issued pursuant to section [26-118 of this code.] \ul 28-207.2; or\par
10. A practice or pattern of failing to adhere to construction waste management and disposal plans in accordance with section 28-105.12.14.\par
\ulnone    b. In addition to any of the powers that may be exercised by the commissioner pursuant to this subchapter and chapter [one] \ul 1\ulnone  of this title, the commissioner, after notice and an opportunity to be heard, may refuse to issue or renew, or may suspend or revoke, a license required under this subchapter if the applicant or licensee, or any of its principals, officers\ul ,\ulnone  or directors, or any of its stockholders owning more than [ten] \ul 10\ulnone  percent of the outstanding stock of the corporation has been convicted of a crime which, in the judgment of the commissioner, has a direct relationship to such person's fitness or ability to perform any of the activities for which a license is required under this subchapter or has been convicted of any other crime which, in accordance with article [twenty-three-a] \ul 23-a\ulnone  of the correction law, would provide a justification for the commissioner to refuse to issue or renew, or to suspend or revoke, such license.\par
\'a7 2. Section 28-101.5 of the administrative code of the city of New York is amended by adding new definitions \ldblquote CONSTRUCTION AND DEMOLITION WASTE\rdblquote  and \ldblquote LICENSED HAULER\rdblquote  in alphabetical order to read as follows:\par
\pard\ltrpar\qj\ul\b CONSTRUCTION AND DEMOLITION WASTE.\b0  The non-putrescible waste products resulting from building demolition, construction, alteration, and excavation, including but not limited to dirt, earth, plaster, concrete, rock, rubble, slag, ashes, and waste timber and lumber.\par
\b\par
LICENSED HAULER.\b0  Any business or entity with a valid trade waste removal license issued pursuant to chapter 1 of title 16-a of this code.\par
\par
\pard\ltrpar\fi720\sl480\slmult1\qj\ulnone\'a7 3. Article 105 of chapter 1 of title 28 of the administrative code of the city of New York is amended by adding a new section 28-105.12.14 to read as follows:\par
\pard\ltrpar\qj\ul\b\'a7 28-105.12.14 Construction waste management and disposal plan required.\b0  As part of an application for the construction of a new building or alteration or demolition of an existing building, the applicant shall submit to the department a construction waste management and disposal plan. Such plan shall include steps to be taken for the disposal of construction and demolition waste, including but not limited to identifying licensed haulers, identifying disposal or recycling facilities to receive such construction and demolition waste, routes licensed haulers will use to transport construction and demolition waste to disposal and recycling facilities, and an estimate of construction and demolition waste to be generated by the work. An applicant may amend a construction waste management and disposal plan submitted to the department pursuant to section 28-104.3.\ulnone  \par
\par
\pard\ltrpar\li360\qj\ul\b\'a7 28-105.12.14.1 Verification prior to permit sign-off.\b0  As part of the sign-off requirements for a permit, the permit holder shall submit to the department documentation to demonstrate compliance with the construction waste management and disposal plan submitted pursuant to section 28-105.12.14, including but not limited to licensed hauler receipts and licensed facility manifests. The department, in conjunction with the department of sanitation and department of environmental protection, shall verify the permit holder\rquote s compliance with the construction waste management and disposal plan prior to permit sign-off.\par
\pard\ltrpar\qj\par
\pard\ltrpar\fi720\sl480\slmult1\qj\ulnone\'a7 4. Section 28-202.1 of the administrative code of the city of New York is amended by adding a new exception 14 to read as follows:\par
\ul 14. The minimum civil penalty for a violation of section 28-105.12.14 shall be $1,000.\par
\ulnone\'a7 5. Section 28-401.19 of the administrative code of the city of New York, as amended by local law number 128 for the year 2024, is amended to read as follows:\par
\pard\ltrpar\hyphpar0\sa240\qj\b\'a7 28-401.19 Suspension or revocation of license or certificate of competence.\expndtw-15  \expndtw1\b0 The commissioner shall have the power to suspend or revoke a license or certificate of competence\ul ,\ulnone  [and/or] to impose a fine not to exceed $25,000 for each finding of violation, [and/or] \ul or\ulnone  to order any holder thereof to repair damage resulting from any act or omission as set forth in this chapter or in rules, \ul or any combination thereof, \ulnone for any of the following:\par
\pard\ltrpar\fi-360\li720\sa240\qj 1.\tab Fraud or deceit in obtaining or renewing a license or seal, certificate of competence, certification, registration, or permit;\expndtw21\b\par
\expndtw1\b0 2.\tab The making of a material false or misleading statement on any form or report filed with the department or other governmental entity;\par
3.\tab The failure to file a statement, report\ul ,\ulnone  or form required by law to be filed;\par
4.\tab Willfully impeding or obstructing the filing of a statement, report\ul ,\ulnone  or form of another required by law to be filed;\par
5.\tab Fraudulent dealings or misrepresentation;\par
6.\tab Negligence, incompetence, lack of knowledge or fitness, or disregard of this code and related laws and rules;\par
7.\tab Failure to comply with this code or any order, rule, or requirement lawfully made by the commissioner including failure to cooperate with investigations related to the trade for which the individual is licensed conducted by the commissioner or other government entity;\par
8.\tab Failure to comply with any order, rule, regulation\ul ,\ulnone  or requirement lawfully made by the commissioner of environmental protection or commissioner of transportation pertaining to water services, house connections, street openings, [street/lane] \ul street or lane\ulnone  closures\ul ,\ulnone  or sidewalk closures that relate to requirements of this code;\par
9.\tab A practice or pattern of failing timely to perform or complete contracts relating to home improvements as defined by section 20-386 [of the administrative code] or a practice of abandoning contracts on residential buildings containing [four] \ul 4\ulnone  dwelling units or less;\par
10.\tab Failure to provide documents, including payroll records, workers compensation or other insurance documents, employee timekeeping records and corporate tax returns, required by the commissioner;\par
11.\tab Engaging or assisting in any act that endangers the public safety and welfare;\par
12.\tab Conviction of a criminal offense where the underlying act arises out of the individual\rquote s professional dealings with the city or any other governmental entity;\par
13.\tab Poor moral character that adversely reflects on [his or her] \ul the individual\rquote s\ulnone  fitness to conduct work regulated by this code; \par
14.\tab Failure to pay outstanding fines, penalties, or fees related to the individual\rquote s professional dealings with the city or any other governmental entity;\par
15.\tab The unauthorized removal of documents from the department;\par
16.\tab Repeated failure to be prepared for or attend scheduled meetings with the department as provided by rule; [or] \par
\expndtw0 17.\tab Failure to notify the department in accordance with section 28-401.18.1[.]\ul ;\ulnone\par
\expndtw1 18. With respect to general contractor registration, upon a finding that the applicant or registrant or a business entity in which [one] \ul 1\ulnone  of the applicant\rquote s or registrant\rquote s principals, officers\ul ,\ulnone  or directors is a principal, officer\ul ,\ulnone  or director has engaged in any of the acts set forth in items 1 through 17 \ul or 21\ulnone  or any of the following:\par
\pard\ltrpar\fi-540\li1260\sa240\qj 18.1.\tab Fraud, misrepresentation\ul ,\ulnone  or bribery in securing a sign-off of work or a temporary or permanent certificate of occupancy[.]\ul ;\par
\ulnone 18.2. A practice on the part of the registrant of failure to timely perform or complete its contracts for the construction of new residential structures containing no more than [three] \ul 3\ulnone  dwelling units, or the manipulation of assets or accounts, or fraud or bad faith[.]\ul ;\par
\ulnone 18.3.\tab Approval or knowledge on the part of the registrant of an act of omission, fraud, or misrepresentation committed by [one] \ul 1\ulnone  or more agents or employees of the registrant, and failure to report such act to the department[.]\ul ;\par
\ulnone  18.4. The applicant or registrant, or any of its principals, officers\ul ,\ulnone  or directors, or any of its stockholders owning more than [ten] \ul 10\ulnone  percent of the outstanding stock of the corporation has been convicted of a crime which, in accordance with article [twenty-three-a] \ul 23-a\ulnone  of the correction law, is determined to have a direct relationship to such person\rquote s fitness or ability to perform any of the activities for which a registration is required under this article[.]\ul ; or\par
\ulnone  18.5. The applicant or registrant, or any of its principals, officers\ul ,\ulnone  or directors has been or is a principal, officer\ul ,\ulnone  or director of a registered general contractor whose registration has been revoked[.]\ul ;\par
\pard\ltrpar\fi-360\li720\qj\expndtw0\ulnone 19. Contract work by holders of special electrician\rquote s licenses[.]\ul ;\par
\pard\ltrpar\fi360\qj\ulnone\par
\pard\ltrpar\fi-360\li720\qj 20. Failure to demonstrate fitness to engage in the trade for which the individual is licensed[.]\ul ; or\par
\pard\ltrpar\fi360\qj\ulnone\par
\pard\ltrpar\fi-360\li720\qj\ul 21. A practice or pattern of failing to adhere to a construction waste management and disposal plan in accordance with section 28-105.12.14.\par
\pard\ltrpar\qj\ulnone\par
\'a7 6. This local law takes effect immediately. \fs18\par
